prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "滑雪跨步" (ski hops / lateral airborne jumps) exercise, front view, facing the viewer, freeze-frame photographic style. Person posture: Photographic freeze-frame, front view, of a lateral ski-style HOP. The athlete is suspended IN MID-AIR — about 15-20 cm of CLEAR EMPTY FLOOR SPACE is visible directly below both feet (a small shadow on the ground far below the body confirms airborne). The body is mid-flight TRAVELING LATERALLY (sideways) to the LEFT side of the frame. BOTH FEET ARE TOGETHER and SWUNG to the LEFT side of the body (feet are about 30 cm to the left of where the head is). The HIPS AND TORSO LEAN to the LEFT, body angled sideways like a downhill skier carving a turn. Both knees slightly bent. Both arms swing to the RIGHT side of the body for counterbalance (opposite to the legs), like a skier's ski poles. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. NEGATIVE: this is NOT a stationary standing pose — the body is AIRBORNE, suspended above the ground, traveling sideways through the air.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps, gluteus maximus, and gluteus medius of both legs for lateral propulsion. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: calf muscles and core muscles for stabilization. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌 / 臀大肌" pointing to the quadriceps and gluteus maximus; "臀中肌" pointing to the gluteus medius for lateral motion; "小腿 / 核心" pointing to the calf and core area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
